reasoning: The US economy grew steadily throughout 2023 and 2024, with solid GDP expansion across all quarters (e.g., 2.3% annualized in Q4 2024). A recession is defined by the NBER Business Cycle Dating Committee as a significant decline in economic activity lasting more than a few months. Given the sustained growth in real GDP, employment, and income through 2023 and 2024, it is virtually impossible that the NBER will retroactively declare a recession for those years by the end of 2025. The market consensus as of May 1, 2025 matches this, pricing the outcome at around 1%. Therefore, the probability is set to the floor of 2%.
